input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
The most likely cause is that you never set the page's **`BindingContext`** to your ViewModel. Without it, `{Binding …}` has nothing to resolve against.

**Fix — set the BindingContext** (pick one approach):

**Option A – Code-behind:**
```csharp
public partial class MyPage : ContentPage
{
    public MyPage()
    {
        InitializeComponent();
        BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
    }
}
```

**Option B – XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements **`INotifyPropertyChanged`** (or inherits `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change after initial load.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank even though the ViewModel has values. Looking at the XAML, the bindings look correct syntactically, but the most likely issue is that the `BindingContext` is never set.

There could also be a secondary issue: the ViewModel properties might not implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, but the user says "properties have values" which suggests they're set, just not showing.

The primary issue is almost certainly the missing `BindingContext`.
